text.insert: object is not free-form: every one of the 14 types has a closed TypeBox state schema. Those schemas, the
facts, and the named actions are owned by seven component bundles under
— a boot-time check asserts the bundles partition the 14 types exactly, and the viewer mirrors the same seven folders 1:1.
A bundle is a manifest (name, owned types, agent-facing description), a state definition per owned type, an action list, and an
projection function. The bundle name doubles as the folder name, the discovery key, and the viewer mirror key.

rows: paragraph, heading, list-item, quote, callout, divider, image, video
The rich-text flow: typing, marks, links, lists, inline embeds.
Source code: language-tagged source text with structured line annotations.
Mermaid diagram: diagram source with live render.
Annotated path tree: entries with notes and change markers.
Structured table: a columns × rows grid.
Operation list describing how a system can be changed or queried.
Spatial canvas. The block holds a reference; the canvas lives in its own system.
props.title: Component bundles

text.insert: A state definition pairs a TypeBox object schema for
fact. The same schema object serves three consumers: runtime validation of action params and writes, verbatim JSON Schema in the discovery payload, and the editor's structured forms.

rows: tone?: info | decision | risk | warning | success; kind?: string; title?: string
src: string; alt?: string; caption?: string
src?: string; url?: string; title?: string; caption?: string
language?: string; annotations?: { lines, label?, note }[]
title?: string; caption?: string; diagramType?: string
title?: string; entries: { path, note?, change?, from? }[]
title?: string; columns: string[]; rows: string[][]; density?: compact | normal | relaxed
title?: string; operations: { name, description?, params?, returns?, kind? }[]
canvasId?: string; src?: string; view?: string; title?: string
props.title: State schema summary

text.insert: . Unknown props are schema violations, not tolerated extras — growth happens by changing the schema, never by smuggling keys. Readers stay tolerant separately: the structured components ship tolerant read helpers that skip malformed entries instead of throwing.
Structured blocks expose named actions so agents can say what they mean — add a row, update an entry — instead of hand-editing props. An action is registry data: a
key, a description, a TypeBox params schema, and a pure
function that returns a shallow-merge props patch. The kernel executes the patch through
, so actions inherit the same merge semantics and inverse-op undo path as every other write.

annotations.label: Registry key
annotations.note: The action key is always <blockType>.<verb>, so the component registry can list and resolve actions without inspecting props.

annotations.label: Discovery params
annotations.note: Params is the action's TypeBox schema, served verbatim as JSON Schema and used for runtime validation.

annotations.label: Undo for free
annotations.note: apply returns a shallow-merge props patch. The kernel executes it through updateBlock, so the same merge semantics and inverse op path apply.

rows: (none — the flow edits through the kernel ops directly)
canvas.* — lifted from the canvas agent schema, forwarded not applied
props.title: Action inventory

text.insert: An action either applies locally or declares
. The canvas bundle lifts the canvas agent-patch operations as
actions whose param schemas stay owned by the canvas package; the docs side routes them to the canvas authority instead of touching block props.

serves static metadata derived entirely from the component and operation exports — nothing hand-maintained. This is the one HTTP-shaped thing on these pages, and only because the payload is itself data: the seven ops with descriptions, then each bundle's types,
facts, state schemas, and actions with verbatim JSON Schema params.

annotations.label: Discovery v2
annotations.note: GET /api/blocks serves static metadata derived entirely from component and operation exports.

annotations.label: Seven ops
annotations.note: ops lists the seven kernel operations with their descriptions.

annotations.label: Component-owned surface
annotations.note: Each bundle reports its types, carriesText facts, state schemas, actions, and verbatim JSON Schema params.
